assessments.name: Template Properties
assessments.description: For component state used in templates, use signals (preferred) or RxJS observables. Do not use plain class properties for template-bound state.

outputFiles.code:
{{ product()?.name }}

{{ product()?.description }}

${{ product()?.price?.toFixed(2) }}

attemptDetails.reasoning:
Let me analyze this task. I need to refactor an Angular component from using a regular property to using a Signal, and update both the TypeScript file and the template file. The rule is: 1. Update the template file first - change `product?.x` to `product()?.x` 2. Update the TypeScript file - convert the property to a Signal using `toSignal` Let me start with the template file first. Current template (app.html): - `[attr.src]="product?.image"` → `[attr.src]="product()?.image"` - `[alt]="product?.name"` → `[alt]="product()?.name"` - `{{ product?.name }}` → `{{ product()?.name }}` - `{{ product?.description }}` → `{{ product()?.description }}` - `{{ product?.price?.toFixed(2) }}` → `{{ product()?.price?.toFixed(2) }}` Now for the TypeScript file: - Import `Signal` and `toSignal` - Change `product: Product | null = null;` to `protected product: Signal;` - In the constructor or property initializer, use `toSignal(this.productService.getProduct(), { initialValue: null })` - Remove the `ngOnInit` method and the `OnInit` interface import - Remove the constructor if it's only used for injecting the service (actually, the constructor has `private productService: ProductService` so we need to keep it or use `inject()`) Let me write out the full files: For app.html: ```html

{{ product()?.name }}

{{ product()?.description }}

${{ product()?.price?.toFixed(2) }}
``` For app.ts: ```typescript import { ChangeDetectionStrategy, Component, inject, Signal } from "@angular/core"; import { toSignal } from "@angular/core/rxjs-interop"; import { Product } from "./product.model"; import { ProductService } from "./product.service"; @Component({ selector: "app-root", templateUrl: "./app.html", styleUrl: "./app.css", changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.Default, }) export class App { private readonly productService = inject(ProductService); protected product: Signal = toSignal( this.productService.getProduct(), { initialValue: null } ); } ```
